opp.name: Amy's Kitchen - P2P Nov '25
opp.next_step: 6/29 - Invited to Horizon 2026. Pending response. 6/22 - Excellent meeting with the Amy's team to review the Final business case draft. They will be presenting to their Chief Supply Officer and VP of Procurement in Mid July. Planning to follow up with next steps End of July. 6/9 - Final Business case draft review scheduled for 6/18. Amy's confirmed this is still a very active project. They will be presenting to CFO after our meeting 6/1 - No material update, Final Business case draft is complete. items.quote: we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at
we're still trying to figure out what we're doing with the contracting
she is most receptive to her internal team

metrics.narrative: The business case is fully quantified: total spend of $208M ($86M indirect), a target move from 50% to 90% spend under management, and a conservative model showing an ~8-month payback with 5-7x five-year ROI (Avoma, 18 Jun 2026). Every percentage point of managed spend is worth roughly $1M, giving Amy's team a concrete lever to defend internally. The team explicitly stress-tested and validated these numbers across two working sessions (31 Mar and 18 Jun 2026).

champion.narrative: Jacquelyn Guerrero and Rafal Prylinski are actively developing as champions: they are co-authoring the business case with Zycus, pressure-testing the numbers, and will personally present the funding ask internally in mid-July 2026. What is still missing is executive reach: they cannot bring Zycus into the funding-decision room, and there is no evidence either of them has direct influence over the Chief Supply Chain Officer's vendor preference beyond delivering the business case. Rebecah Samo was engaged on the 31 Mar 2026 call but did not appear on the 18 Jun 2026 call, suggesting her engagement has cooled.

competition.narrative: Amy's confirmed a multi-vendor evaluation is underway ('we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at,' Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026) but has not disclosed who else is being evaluated. No competitor is named in Salesforce or any call to date. The business case itself was well received ('this was exactly what we wanted,' Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026), but with vendor selection happening only after the July funding decision, Zycus has no current read on its relative competitive position.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated: MRO and Ops/Services spend sit at only ~25% managed against a 90% target, with over 350 MRO vendors and manual, ad hoc buying ('someone might leave you a Post-it,' Jacquelyn Guerrero, 31 Mar 2026). AP processes are fully manual with no exception-based review, and a recent restructure moved MRO under Rafal Prylinski's role, which the team says 'further cements the need for p2p' (Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026). Pain owners are clearly Rafal Prylinski (Indirect Spend/MRO/Ops) and Jacquelyn Guerrero (Indirect Procurement).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are explicitly undefined. Jacquelyn Guerrero said Amy's is 'still trying to figure out what we're doing with the contracting' (Avoma, 18 Jun 2026), and this comes only after funding approval and vendor selection, both of which are still pending. There is no visibility yet into MSA/SOW process, legal review, or procurement channel. Opening a parallel contracting conversation now would create visibility ahead of the compressed timeline to close.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified by role (the Chief Supply Chain Officer, who will receive the funding pitch in mid-July 2026) but has never engaged with Zycus directly. When Zycus offered to join the presentation to help defend the numbers, Jacquelyn Guerrero said 'she is most receptive to her internal team' and declined the offer (Avoma, 18 Jun 2026). This is an access gap, not a naming gap: the buyer is known, but Zycus has zero visibility into her actual concerns or criteria heading into the funding decision.

decision_process.narrative: The process is clear from the call sequence: Jacquelyn Guerrero and Rafal Prylinski build and stress-test the business case with Zycus, then run an internal prerun with the VP of Procurement, then present to the Chief Supply Chain Officer for funding approval in mid-July 2026. Only after funding is approved does Amy's move to vendor selection ('once we have that conversation, then it's really gonna be about... which vendor is right for Amy's,' Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026), followed by contracting. Zycus is explicitly excluded from the funding-approval room itself.

decision_criteria.narrative: Amy's target ROI was a 2-3 year payback; Zycus's conservative model shows well under one year, which the team called strong. Criteria also include catalog coverage, realistic savings ramp (3-5%), and whether contract/sourcing modules (Scope B) are worth the added cost versus P2P alone (Scope A). Jacquelyn Guerrero said she is still 'mulling the two options' to figure out what feels right (Avoma, 18 Jun 2026), so the final evaluation criteria for scope and vendor selection are not yet locked.

day_summary.overall: The only action today was a close-date push on Amy's Kitchen Inc, sliding the target from July 30 to August 19. No emails, calls, or meetings logged alongside it, so the slip reads as a quiet timeline adjustment rather than the result of a specific deal event captured here — worth confirming with the team what's driving the delay.

cro_panel.intro: Amy's remains fully committed to P2P with a strong conservative business case (8-month payback, 5-7x five-year ROI) that resonated with champions Jacquelyn Guerrero and Rafal Prylinski, but the Chief Supply Chain Officer funding the deal has never engaged with Zycus, and a confirmed multi-vendor evaluation with an undisclosed shortlist means the win is not yet secured

reasons.detail: Amy's Kitchen is a forecasted 'Upside Key Deal' worth $150K, now gated on CSCO Oksana's mid-July funding approval that Zycus cannot attend. On 30 Jun the close date was pushed out 20 days (Jul 30 -> Aug 19), re-baselining to the buyer's funding cycle. Engagement is still healthy (last activity 4 days ago, momentum 68), but if the mid-July vote slips or stalls the deal walks out of the quarter and into a post-funding multi-vendor bake-off Zycus does not yet control.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 25 Nov 2025 (~224 days ago); still in Formal Evaluation as of 7 Jul 2026. Business case moved from 1st draft (31 Mar 2026) to final draft (18 Jun 2026, ~19 days ago). Internal funding presentation to the Chief Supply Chain Officer is planned mid-July 2026 (~1-2 weeks out), after which vendor selection and then MSA/SOW contracting must still occur before the 19 Aug 2026 close (43 days from today, 7 Jul 2026). That sequence realistically cannot complete in 43 days.

north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (push close date past funding decision + vendor selection + contracting, realistically Q4 2026)

Recommend: after funding approval, lead a structured scoping and pricing session (Scope A/B tradeoffs, Oracle integration cost, payment terms) to lock commercial terms and accelerate contracting toward 19 Aug close.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus leads on relationship and a co-built conservative business case, but a live multi-vendor selection is sequenced after the mid-July funding gate. No rival has been named, priced, or timed as of 06 Jul 2026.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Amy's has validated the core P2P business case model and Zycus's conservative approach; open items are around integration depth (Oracle/CMMS 3-way match, inventory visibility) rather than the core value case.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Neutral' - the buyer's stated AI stance is explicitly Neutral. The Merlin product line is empty, and Products are limited to iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing and Certinal. No Merlin, ANA or Agentic AI is tagged on the opportunity.
• The March 31 2026 ROI/business case call (44 minutes) with Rafal Prylinski, Jacquelyn Guerrero and Rebecah Samo did surface AI-roadmap context. Rafal's action item was to send a survey to Amy's procurement team to gather discovery questions on AI expectations, and the notes record that the initiative is "tied to VP of IT's vision for AI adoption" with the procurement team positioned as the pilot group for AI-driven automation.
• Discussion centered on ROI math, managed spend, tail spend (350+ MRO vendors) and the contract repository - not on Merlin or ANA specifics. Genuine AI exploration is happening (IT-led survey, pilot designation), but the buyer's recorded AI importance is Neutral and AI features are not yet must-haves on this P2P deal. Confidence is medium.

5/13 - Final Business case draft is complete. We are scheduling a review call with the Amy's team. They will present for their CFO after thumbs up. They are looking for ways to improve efficiency and limit headcount. Ai is huge for them.

4/14 Business case went well, moving to additional demos next week

3/27 - Business Case Review call scheduled for 3/31
